title: betula alba var. bircalensis
intro: Betula alba var. bircalensis, also known as the White Birch, is a deciduous tree or shrub native to the mountainous regions of Turkey and the Middle East. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, making it effective in reducing inflammation and pain. The health benefits of Betula alba var. bircalensis include its ability to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and act as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments, including colds, flu, and skin conditions.
